English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Mortar Board Honor Society records
Mortar Board (Society). Hexenkreis Chapter (Cornell University)
37-4-2199
2.6 cubic feet. 2.6 cubic feet.
1919-2006. Correspondence, a minute book, an audiotape, and other records. Early records of the organization, a national senior honor society for women. Letter and tape recorded reminiscences of Lulu S. Howard and letter from Lois Osborn, both to Marjorie E. Lasko of Ohio State University for a Mortar Board history project, 1984. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 4 ======= 4 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2079277
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M. Moran
Date completed:
July 1985
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, February 2004
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Mortar Board is a national senior honor society for women.
Cite As:
Mortar Board Honor Society records, #37-4-2199. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Early records of the organization. Includes a copy of a letter from Caroline H. Swartout to Miss Osborn about the society and a typescript four-page history, 1938. Also a minute book, 1919-1930. Letter and tape recorded reminiscences of Lulu S. Howard and letter from Lois Osborn, both to Marjorie E. Lasko of Ohio State University for a Mortar Board history project, 1984.
Scope and content
Recent records of the organization include publicity for lectures and other events, project evaluation forms, planning calendars, and Executive Board agendas and meeting minutes.
Scope and content
Also: record of activities, yearly report, Cornell Sun articles, minutes, agendas, announcements.
Women college students. Cornell University -- : Societies, etc.
